title: Interactive To-Do List 📝
description: Use the DOM, Arrays, and Event Listeners to build a working To-Do list application.

tasks.instruction: Use `document.querySelector` to select the input field with the id '#task-input' and store it in a variable named `inputField`.

validationRules.errorMessage: Make sure to use document.querySelector with '#task-input'.

tasks.instruction: Use `document.querySelector` to select the button with the id '#add-btn' and store it in a variable named `addBtn`.
tasks.initialCode:
const inputField = document.querySelector('#task-input');
// Select the button here

validationRules.errorMessage: Make sure to use document.querySelector with '#add-btn'.

tasks.instruction: Create an empty array named `tasks` to hold the list items.

validationRules.errorMessage: Create an empty array using let or const named tasks.

tasks.instruction: Add a 'click' event listener to `addBtn` using an arrow function. Inside it, just `console.log("Clicked!")` for now.

validationRules.errorMessage: Ensure you add a 'click' listener to addBtn that logs 'Clicked!' using an arrow function.
